_meta.purpose: Curated opening positions for the chess-dashboard Openings Library section. Each entry shows the key starting position that defines the opening (the moment after which it becomes recognizable). Levy Rozman's recommended sub-1500 picks are flagged with is_levy_pick.
_meta.fen_format: After the listed moves; the side_to_move tells whose turn it is in the displayed position
_meta.source: Levy Rozman, How to Win at Chess (Ten Speed Press, 2023) — see knowledge/chess/openings/ atoms

openings.name: London System
openings.summary: Universal d4 system. Bishop comes out to f4 BEFORE getting blocked. Same plan against almost anything Black plays — Levy's #1 pick for sub-1500.

openings.name: Vienna System
openings.summary: Levy's e4 weapon — 70% personal win rate. Sets up the Vienna Gambit (3.f4) against ...Nf6. Off the beaten path; opponents rarely know it.

openings.name: Alapin Sicilian (anti-Sicilian for White)
openings.summary: Sidesteps 90% of Sicilian theory by playing 2.c3 instead of 2.Nf3. Prepares d4. Removes opponent's prep advantage.

openings.name: Italian Game
openings.summary: Classical e4 opening, named after 17th-c. Italian master Greco. Bishop aims at f7 (Black's weakest square). Solid, principled.

openings.name: Ruy López (Spanish Opening)
openings.summary: The most-studied opening at master level. Bishop attacks the c6 knight defending the e5 pawn. Carlsen's go-to — but theory-heavy.

openings.name: Queen's Gambit
openings.summary: Classic d4 opening offering the c-pawn. Black usually declines (QGD). Got mainstream attention via the Netflix series.

openings.name: Caro-Kann Defense
openings.summary: Levy's PERSONAL favorite — beat 100+ GMs with it. Building-block defense (c6 supports d5). Solid pawn structure. Drops White's win rate from 51% to 47% at sub-1600.

openings.name: Scandinavian Defense
openings.summary: Force White to react on move 1. Queen out early (the exception that proves the rule). Memorize: Qd5 → Qa5 → c6 (queen escape).

openings.name: Sicilian Defense
openings.summary: Most popular Black response at GM level. Asymmetric counter-attack. Theory-dense (Najdorf, Dragon, Sveshnikov, etc.) — Levy doesn't recommend for sub-1500.

openings.name: French Defense
openings.summary: Building-block sister to Caro-Kann (e6 supports d5 instead of c6). Creates closed positions. Light-squared bishop is the problem child.

openings.name: Queen's Gambit Declined (setup)
openings.summary: Levy's pick vs d4 (and other slow openings). Decline the c4 pawn — accepting bumps White's win rate to 56%. Setup transposes to London, English, Reti.

openings.name: King's Indian Defense (KID)
openings.summary: Setup-based defense playable against most White openings. Kasparov's favorite. Counterpunching style — closed positions, kingside pawn storms.
